Title: The elicitor VP2 from Verticillium dahliae triggers defence response in cotton

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots for JA/SA measurement; stem/hypocotyl vascular tissues for lignin deposition
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant defence and resistance to Verticillium wilt
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Heterologous expression of VP2 in cotton increases JA, SA, and lignin accumulation and enhances resistance to Verticillium dahliae without obvious growth defects
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: secreted cysteine-rich elicitor protein with N-terminal signal peptide; no known functional domain reported
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plant plasma membrane when full length; VP2 without signal peptide localizes to plasma membrane and nucleus in Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly induced early during infection in defoliating isolates V991 and T9 at 6 dpi; very low in non-defoliating isolates 1cd3-2 and BP2 during invasion
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transgenic cotton, high transcript accumulation in roots and leaves
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Verticillium dahliae infection/inoculation; uninoculated normal growth conditions; transient agroinfiltration; recombinant protein infiltration
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for transient expression in N. benthamiana; overexpression construct in transgenic cotton promoter not clearly stated
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: comparative transcriptome plus secretome screening; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana and Arabidopsis; recombinant protein infiltration in cotton; VIGS dependency assay for NbBAK1/NbSOBIR1; fungal knockout and complementation; stable overexpression in cotton; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; hormone measurement by HPLC-MS/MS; lignin staining and quantification
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VP2 was identified from the V991 secretome/transcriptome as a secreted elicitor specifically upregulated during early infection by the defoliating pathotype. 2. Full-length VP2, but not signal-peptide-deleted VP2, triggered cell death and defence marker expression in multiple plant species. 3. VP2-induced cell death required NbBAK1 but not NbSOBIR1. 4. Deletion of VP2 reduced V. dahliae virulence, whereas complementation restored pathogenicity. 5. Heterologous overexpression of VP2 in cotton elevated JA, SA, and lignin-related defence responses and improved Verticillium wilt resistance without detectable growth penalty.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: VP2 is only found in Verticillium spp. according to BLAST analysis; sequence is highly conserved between defoliating and non-defoliating pathotypes, with two amino acid differences in the signal peptide that do not alter secretion; likely perceived at the plant cell surface as a PAMP-like elicitor, but the cognate cotton receptor was not identified
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious abnormalities in plant growth and development were observed in VP2-overexpressing cotton under greenhouse conditions
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: VP2 is a promising pathogen-derived elicitor for engineering Verticillium wilt resistance in cotton with minimal growth penalty; useful for transgenic deployment or possibly elicitor-based immune priming, though receptor identity and broader agronomic effects need further study
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, GSA CRA011235 and CRA011237; label-free secretome proteomics accession NA
